## **Brahms** Two String Quartets, Op 51

**Zehetmair Quartet**(ECM New Series)

Familiar repertoire made to feel urgently fresh, from an ensemble – albeit with two changes – that won Recording of the Year in 2002 with an equally compelling album of Schumann.

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/sm4pyvak/beethoven-piano-sonatas-nos-28-32.png)**

## **Beethoven** Piano Sonatas Nos 28-32

**Paul Wee** _pf_(BIS)

Having hugely impressed in virtuosic but lesser-recorded repertoire, pianist Paul Wee now demonstrates brilliance of colour and technique in Beethoven’s final sonatas, the most core of repertoire.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/beethoven-the-last-piano-sonatas-paul-wee)

## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/hkxdoqb4/beethoven-sciarrino.jpg)**

## **Beethoven** Piano Concerto No 4 **Sciarrino** Il Sogno di Stradella

**Jonathan Biss** _pf_ **Swedish RSO / Malin Broman; Omer Meir Wellber**(Orchid)

Jonathan Biss’s ongoing survey of Beethoven’s piano concertos – this time No 4 – paired with a modern companion offers another superb instalment.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/beethoven-piano-concerto-no-4-sciarrino-il-sogno-di-stradella-jonathan-biss)

source.subsection:
## **![](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/media/o0wpkv3r/gregson-vision.jpg)**

## **Gregson** A Vision in a Dream

**BBC Philharmonic Orchestra /** **Ben Gernon**(Chandos)

Concertos for tuba, viola and oboe plus further orchestral works – Edward Gregson’s sound world unfolds beautifully over an album played with devotion.

[**Read the _Gramophone_ review**](https://www.gramophone.co.uk/review/gregson-a-vision-in-a-dream-concertos)
